biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:STABLE — 11/12 tool-using units (92%; units 4,6,8,21,25,32,34,35,40,45,46); ls-via-Bash head/tail split (unit 4), grep-rln-via-Bash and rg-l-via-Bash x2 and rg-via-Bash x3+ and cat|grep pipeline on package.json (unit 4), grep-n-via-Bash and rg-via-Bash as section anchor and wc-l-via-Bash (unit 6), rg-via-Bash x3+ as section anchors between Edit calls (unit 8), rg-l-via-Bash and for-loop-rg-via-Bash (unit 21), ls-via-Bash (unit 25), find-via-Bash and /usr/bin/grep explicit-path x6+ (unit 32), cat-n-via-Bash and /usr/bin/grep explicit-path x4+ and for-loop-Bash success=false (unit 34), /usr/bin/grep explicit-path x3+ and find-via-Bash (unit 35), grep-rln-via-Bash and /usr/bin/grep x4+ (unit 40), grep-rn-via-Bash and grep-rln-via-Bash x2 and ls-via-Bash and /usr/bin/grep x5+ (unit 45), /usr/bin/grep x6+ and xargs-grep-pipeline success=false (unit 46); rate 92% matches prior cycle peak exactly; zero internalization across 25+ consecutive measured cycles; structurally identical to all prior cycles with no change in violation type or frequency

re_edit_thrash:RECURRING:STABLE — units 6,8,32; unit 6: 10+ Edit calls on plan.md with interleaved rg-via-Bash section anchors and Reads; unit 8: Edit(success=false) then rg-via-Bash anchor then Read then 7+ Edit calls on plan.md with interleaved rg anchors; unit 32: 4 Edit calls on data-history-v3.tsx plus 6 Edit calls on data-history-v3.table.tsx plus 3 Edit calls on s1-upload.tsx; peak Edit count per file unchanged from all prior measured cycles; no improvement trend across 10+ consecutive cycles; document-editing thrash on plan.md co-occurs with shell_tool_preference_violations as section-locating strategy

path_assumption_bias:RECURRING:WORSENING — units 45 and 46; second consecutive session with confirmed intra-session double-repeat of Read on use-sync-q.ts (success=false on both units 45 and 46); prior cycle (2026-05-07) noted this as FIRST CONFIRMED intra-session double-repeat; this cycle confirms the pattern is repeating across sessions, not an isolated incident; correct path src/utils/hooks/use-query-sync/index.ts successfully read in unit 46 after failures confirming agent can locate correct path but defaults to wrong one at turn start; cross-session internalization remains zero across 16+ consecutive sessions; pattern now escalating from single-instance per cycle to double-instance per cycle across two consecutive sessions

optimistic_edit_without_read:RECURRING:STABLE — unit 8: Edit on plan.md (success=false) before rg-via-Bash anchor + Read + successful Edit; unit 34: Edit on file-select.v2.tsx (success=false) before /usr/bin/grep anchor + successful Edit; two instances consistent with all prior seven measured cycles at 1-2 instances per cycle; no improvement trend across 9+ consecutive cycles

rm_instead_of_trash:RECURRING:ESCALATING — unit 34: Bash rm -f /tmp/fs-tests/count-searches.ts (success=false, blocked by hook) followed by correct trash recovery; prior cycle (2026-05-07) flagged this as NEW:SINGLE-OCCURRENCE and noted to monitor for recurrence; this cycle confirms recurrence; now classified RECURRING; self-corrected within same unit in both occurrences but the initial violation persists despite hook block in prior cycle; CLAUDE.md rule states trash not rm and hook blocks unconditionally; two occurrences across two consecutive sessions qualifies for recurring classification

cross_references.note: rm-then-trash recovery and Edit(success=false)-then-grep-then-success pattern both represent acting on assumed state (file content, file path) rather than verified state; partial violation of the assertion-without-observation meta-rule

cross_references.insight: Context reconstruction after session boundaries is a data-hallucination risk (conf=0.55)
cross_references.note: use-sync-q.ts path failure in both units 45 and 46, same session, confirms the agent is reconstructing a stale path from prior-session context rather than verifying current state; directly violates the insight's recommended behavior of flagging unverified state assumptions after catchup

cross_references.insight: Terse input = terse output, context-budget signal (conf=0.61)
cross_references.note: unit 46 input was 210 chars; output was 13582 chars with 5 code blocks; response length significantly exceeded input brevity signal; not a severe violation but inconsistent with the insight's guidance

recommendations: shell_tool_preference_violations has shown zero internalization across 25+ consecutive cycles — hook-level enforcement is the only remaining lever; no further calibration recommendations are meaningful for this bias as it has been flagged in every prior cycle without change

path_assumption_bias is worsening: intra-session double-repeat of use-sync-q.ts path failure now confirmed across two consecutive sessions; the agent successfully locates the correct path after failure each time, confirming the knowledge exists but is not applied at turn-start; recommend adding a project-level memory entry pinning the correct path for this file as an immediate mitigation

rm_instead_of_trash has graduated from NEW to RECURRING across two consecutive sessions; recommend hook review — if the block is firing correctly but the violation still occurs, consider a pre-commit hook or a stronger interrupt pattern; the self-correction is positive but the initial violation recurs

re_edit_thrash on plan.md is structurally coupled to shell_tool_preference_violations as a section-locating strategy; fixing the shell tool bias would likely reduce thrash on document edits; treating them as independent recommendations has not produced improvement
